title: docker compose build with podman ignores git branch
body:
### Issue Description

When building an image with [docker/compose](https://github.com/docker/compose) with podman as the docker command and with a git context, the specified branch is ignored and the cache from the initial build is always used.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Use the following `compose.yaml`:
```yaml
services:
  cfn-lint:
    build:
      context: https://github.com/aws-cloudformation/cfn-lint.git#v1.22.4
```
2. `docker compose build`
3. `docker compose run --rm cfn-lint --version` will output `cfn-lint 1.29.1` (the latest version)


### Describe the results you received

Changing the git branch is always ignored.
Even later, if the repository has evolved and the branch is modified, the cache is always used.
One has to use `--no-cache` to force a rebuild with the latest version (and not the specified branch).
Building again without `--no-cache` will again use the layers of the **initial build**.

### Describe the results you expected

I expect the git branch to be taken into account.

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Docker Compose version 2.33.0

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
I made the assumption that the Dockerfile from the `cfn-lint` project was using the repository contents to build `cfn-lint`, the assumption was false:
```Dockerfile
FROM public.ecr.aws/docker/library/python:3.13-alpine3.20

RUN pip install cfn-lint[full]
RUN pip install pydot

ENTRYPOINT ["cfn-lint"]
CMD ["--help"]
```

The bug is invalid, closing...
